# systemd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `systemd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `systemd.user.automounts` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd automount unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user automount units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the user systemd service manager. |
| `systemd.user.mounts` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd mount unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user mount units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.packages` | `list of package` | Packages providing systemd user units. |
| `systemd.user.paths` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd path unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user path units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.services` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd service unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user service units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.servicesStartTimeoutMs` | `unsigned integer, meaning >=0` | How long to wait for started services to fail until their start is considered successful. The value 0 indicates no timeout. |
| `systemd.user.sessionVariables` | `attribute set of (signed integer or string)` | Environment variables that will be set for the user session. The variable values must be as described in environment.d (5) . |
| `systemd.user.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string) or a list of them for duplicate keys)` | Extra config options for user session service manager. See systemd-user.conf (5) for available options. |
| `systemd.user.slices` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd slice unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user slice units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.sockets` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd socket unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user socket units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.startServices` | `boolean or one of “suggest”, “sd-switch”` | Whether new or changed services that are wanted by active targets should be started. Additionally, stop obsolete services from the previous generation. |
| `systemd.user.systemctlPath` | `string` | Absolute path to the systemctl tool. This option may need to be set if running Home Manager on a non-NixOS distribution. |
| `systemd.user.targets` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd target unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user target units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.timers` | `attribute set of (open submodule of systemd timer unit configuration)` | Definition of systemd per-user timer units. Attributes are merged recursively. |
| `systemd.user.tmpfiles.rules` | `list of string` | Rules for creating and cleaning up temporary files automatically. See tmpfiles.d (5) for the exact format. |
